# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 10

This is turn 10. Turns 1 through 9 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 10.

## Turn 10 — all nontrivial simple Frattini modules close uniformly

Turn 9 left higher-dimensional nontrivial simple \(\mathbf F_2\)-modules as the next possible source of failure.  I retain Candidate \(A_2\), with the prescribed \(\omega_2=40491355905\). 

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The implicit relation remains \(\tau^\sigma=\tau^2\).

### Uniform coefficient-duality theorem

Let \(\Gamma_{A_2}\) be the presented group. Let \(V\) be any nontrivial simple finite \(\mathbf F_2[\Gamma_{A_2}]\)-module whose action factors through a finite quotient.

Then

\[
H^0(\Gamma_{A_2},V)=H^2(\Gamma_{A_2},V)=0,
\qquad
\dim H^1(\Gamma_{A_2},V)=\dim V,
\]

and

\[
|Z^1(\Gamma_{A_2},V)|=|V|^2.
\]

Moreover, the coefficient cup product

\[
H^1(\Gamma_{A_2},V)\times
H^1(\Gamma_{A_2},V^\vee)
\longrightarrow
H^2(\Gamma_{A_2},\mathbf F_2)\simeq\mathbf F_2
\]

is perfect.

This holds for every dimension and every tame character, not only the natural two-dimensional \(S_3\)-module treated in Turns 6–8.

### 1. Every simple module is a tame module

In any finite quotient \(G\), the normal 2-group \(O_2(G)\) acts trivially on every simple \(\mathbf F_2[G]\)-module: a finite 2-group in characteristic two has nonzero fixed vectors, and normality makes that fixed subspace \(G\)-stable.

Thus \(x_0,x_1\) act trivially on \(V\). Write \(S,T\) for the actions of \(\sigma,\tau\). They satisfy

\[
S^{-1}TS=T^2,
\]

and \(T\) has odd order \(e\).

Put

\[
P=1+T+\cdots+T^{e-1}.
\]

Because \(e\) is odd, \(P\) is the projector onto \(V^T\). The relation \(S^{-1}TS=T^2\) makes \(V^T\) \(S\)-stable. Simplicity therefore gives the dichotomy

\[
P=0
\quad\text{or}\quad
P=1.
\]

In the second case \(T=1\).

If \(P=0\), then \(1+T\) is invertible. If \(P=1\), then \(V\) is a nontrivial simple \(S\)-module, so \(1+S^{-1}\) is invertible.

### 2. The coefficient Jacobian always has full rank

Let \(a,b,c,d\in V\) be the cocycle values on \(\sigma,\tau,x_0,x_1\). Since \(c_0\) is a commutator, it has no linear term. The two relation defects are therefore

\[
\begin{aligned}
L_{\mathrm{tame}}
  &=(1+T)a+\bigl(1+S(1+T)\bigr)b,\\
L_{\mathrm{wild}}
  &=Pb+(P+S^{-1})d.
\end{aligned}
\tag{1}
\]

The \(x_0\)-coordinate \(c\) does not occur.

If \(P=0\), the first equation is surjective through \(a\), because \(1+T\) is invertible, and the second is surjective through \(d\), because \(S^{-1}\) is invertible.

If \(P=1\), the two outputs are

\[
b,\qquad b+(1+S^{-1})d,
\]

and again give a surjection \(V^4\to V^2\).

Thus the relation Jacobian has rank \(2\dim V\). Consequently

\[
\dim Z^1=4\dim V-2\dim V=2\dim V.
\]

Since \(V\) is nontrivial simple, \(H^0=0\), so \(\dim B^1=\dim V\) and

\[
\dim H^1=\dim V.
\]

Full surjectivity onto both relator coordinates also forces \(H^2(\Gamma_{A_2},V)=0\) through the relation-module five-term sequence.

Because \(c\) is unrestricted and coboundaries have zero \(x_0\)-coordinate, evaluation on \(x_0\) gives a canonical identification

\[
H^1(\Gamma_{A_2},V)\simeq V.
\tag{2}
\]

### 3. Exact cup-product formula

Let \(S_2=S^{\omega_2}\). Under (2), let \(c\in V\) and \(\lambda\in V^\vee\) represent two coefficient classes supported on \(x_0\).

The \(R_0\) portion of the wild relation contributes

\[
\lambda(c)
\]

through the term \(x_0^2\).

For the correction \(c_0=[d_0,z_0]\), the first-order values are

\[
d_0:\;(P+1)c,
\qquad
z_0:\;S_2^{-1}c.
\]

Hence:

- If \(P=1\), the linear value of \(d_0\) is zero, so the correction contributes nothing.
- If \(P=0\), the commutator contributes

\[
\lambda(S_2c)+\lambda(S_2^{-1}c).
\]

Therefore the complete pairing is

\[
\langle c,\lambda\rangle_{A_2}
=
\begin{cases}
\lambda(c), & T=1,\\[2mm]
\lambda\!\left((1+S_2+S_2^{-1})c\right), & V^T=0.
\end{cases}
\tag{3}
\]

The second operator is always invertible. Indeed, \(S_2\) has 2-power order and

\[
1+S_2+S_2^{-1}
=
S_2^{-1}(S_2^2+S_2+1).
\]

Over \(\mathbf F_2\),

\[
X^{2^m}-1=(X+1)^{2^m},
\]

while \(X^2+X+1\) is coprime to \(X+1\). Thus \(S_2^2+S_2+1\), and hence (3), is invertible.

This proves perfect coefficient duality for every nontrivial simple tame module.

### 4. Global Frattini consequence

Consider a minimal Frattini extension

\[
1\longrightarrow V\longrightarrow \widetilde G
\longrightarrow G\longrightarrow1
\]

in which \(V\) is nontrivial simple.

For both \(\Gamma_{A_2}\) and \(G_{\mathbf Q_2}\),

\[
H^2(-,V)=0,\qquad |Z^1(-,V)|=|V|^2.
\]

Thus every epimorphism to \(G\) has exactly \(|V|^2\) lifts to \(\widetilde G\). Since \(V\subseteq\Phi(\widetilde G)\), every such lift remains surjective.

Therefore:

> **A nontrivial simple Frattini module, of any dimension and any tame character, can never be the first verifier discrepancy.**

Any minimal failing quotient must have final chief factor the trivial module \(\mathbf F_2\), hence must be a central \(C_2\)-extension.

This replaces all future dimension-by-dimension noncentral-module testing.

### 5. Every filtration-one central obstruction is balanced

Now suppose a central class above a nontrivial simple layer has nonzero Lyndon–Hochschild–Serre edge

\[
\gamma\in H^1(G,V^\vee).
\]

Over a fixed base epimorphism, the lifts form an affine space under \(Z^1(\Gamma,V)\). If \(\rho_z\) denotes the lift obtained from a cocycle \(z\), its obstruction has the form

\[
o(\rho_z)
=
o(\rho_0)+
\left\langle [z],f^*\gamma\right\rangle .
\tag{4}
\]

Inflation in degree one is injective, so \(f^*\gamma\neq0\). Perfectness of (3) makes (4) a nonzero affine functional on the \(\dim V\)-dimensional space \(H^1(\Gamma,V)\).

Consequently exactly half of the lifts are obstructed and half lift:

\[
\frac{|V|^2}{2}
\quad\text{versus}\quad
\frac{|V|^2}{2}.
\tag{5}
\]

Thus all genuinely filtration-one central coordinates are now closed uniformly, generalizing the \(8{:}8\) calculation for the Turn-8 \(\beta\)-class.

### 6. What central classes can still survive

For an elementary abelian kernel \(V\),

\[
H^2(V,\mathbf F_2)\simeq \operatorname{Sym}^2(V^\vee).
\]

The square submodule is a copy of \(V^\vee\). Since \(V\) is nontrivial simple,

\[
(V^\vee)^G=0.
\]

Therefore the invariant restriction classes inject into

\[
(\Lambda^2V^\vee)^G.
\tag{6}
\]

Any nonzero invariant alternating form on a simple module is automatically nondegenerate: its radical is a stable submodule. Hence the only unresolved restriction-type classes occur when

\[
V\simeq V^\vee
\]

and \(\dim V\) is even.

So after this turn the only new obstruction type arising over a nontrivial simple layer is a self-dual determinant-type class—the uniform higher-dimensional analogue of the \(C_3\)-natural-module class handled in Turn 7. All non-self-dual branches are closed through their next central layer.

### Exact finite checks

The actual candidate word—not a hard-coded quadratic formula—was evaluated in a cup-integrating semidirect product. The coefficient Jacobian and cup matrix were checked on irreducible tame modules of dimensions \(2,3,4,8\), together with a nontrivial unramified module:

| Module | \(\dim V\) | \(\operatorname{ord}(T)\) | \(\operatorname{ord}(S_2)\) | Jacobian rank | Cup rank |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| inertia \(e=3\) | 2 | 3 | 2 | 4 | 2 |
| inertia \(e=7\) | 3 | 7 | 1 | 6 | 3 |
| inertia \(e=5\) | 4 | 5 | 4 | 8 | 4 |
| inertia \(e=17\) | 8 | 17 | 8 | 16 | 8 |
| unramified order \(7\) | 3 | 1 | 1 | 6 | 3 |

Each computed cup matrix was exactly \(I\) or \(I+S_2+S_2^{-1}\), as predicted.

[Turn 10 uniform coefficient-duality certificate](sandbox:/mnt/data/turn10_uniform_coefficient_duality_certificate.py)

**ESTABLISHED**: Proved perfect coefficient duality for Candidate \(A_2\) for every nontrivial simple \(\mathbf F_2\)-module factoring through an arbitrary finite tame quotient. Derived \(H^2=0\), \(\dim H^1=\dim V\), and exactly \(|V|^2\) lifts for every such Frattini embedding problem. Consequently no nontrivial simple module of any dimension can be the first discrepancy. Proved that every filtration-one central obstruction has an exact \(50{:}50\) split. Reduced all remaining restriction-type obstructions over nontrivial simple layers to invariant alternating forms on self-dual even-dimensional modules.

**GAP**: Candidate \(A_2\) has not been run against the official 5402-group verifier. The remaining genuinely new noncentral-branch obstruction is the \(E_\infty^{0,2}\) determinant-type class for self-dual tame simple modules; purely central \(C_2\)-branches outside those already closed also remain.

**NEXT**: Classify self-dual simple tame modules by the inversion condition on their inertia characters—equivalently \(2^j\equiv-1\pmod d\)—and compute the universal quadratic obstruction attached to their invariant alternating form. The goal is an odd-index determinant-transfer theorem valid in every dimension, closing the entire remaining \(E_\infty^{0,2}\) family at once.
